title: Codex as primary coding agent
context: Needed a dedicated coding muscle for building internal tools and infrastructure
decision: Set up Codex via ACP with OpenAI API key as the default coding agent
reasoning: Highest leverage addition — dedicated coding agent for building tools, automations, and prototypes

title: Hold on Grok/xAI integration
context: Grok offers real-time X/Twitter data for social trend monitoring
decision: Defer until a social monitoring project is active
reasoning: No active project requiring social data. Will revisit when relevant.

title: Company-mirroring agent architecture
context: Needed a scalable agent structure that maps to business functions
decision: Designed 5-department hierarchy: Growth, Fulfilment, Client Success, Operations, Research & Intelligence with QA and Knowledge layers
reasoning: Mirrors real company org structure — easier to scale, reason about, and align with how the business operates

title: Phase 1: Boss + Codex only
context: Full agent architecture defined but workload doesn't yet justify multiple persistent agents
decision: Start with Boss playing all roles, promote agents when workload demands it
reasoning: Don't over-engineer on day one. Earn the complexity through volume of work.

title: Self-hosted AI cloner over Replit
context: Replit browser automation was fragile and unreliable. No public API for Replit Agent.
decision: Built self-hosted AI pipeline: scrape → AI generate → inject widgets → deploy. Dropped Replit entirely.
reasoning: Full control, no third-party dependency, faster execution (30s vs minutes), easier to maintain and scale.

title: Strategic partnership working model
context: Defining how Boss and Orien collaborate
decision: Proactive autonomous collaborator. Overnight work during 19:00-08:00 GMT+1. Morning reports + WhatsApp summary.
reasoning: Orien wants to wake up to progress. Internal work = autonomous, external-facing = ask first.

title: Model-per-agent cost strategy
context: Running all agents on Opus is expensive and unnecessary for routine tasks
decision: Boss stays on Opus (strategic work). Sub-agents use Sonnet (analysis/reasoning) or Haiku (execution). Each agent spec defines its model.
reasoning: Like staffing — senior operator (Opus) coordinates, specialists (Sonnet/Haiku) execute defined tasks. 5-75x cost savings on sub-agents.

title: Meta Ads AI Agent — 4-phase build
context: Orien wants fully autonomous Meta Ads management inspired by a 7-figure autonomous ad agent
decision: Phase 1: Analyst (daily reports) → Phase 2: Creative Engine (Google Drive + AI gen) → Phase 3: Auto-Publisher → Phase 4: Feedback Loop (Fathom)
reasoning: Each phase delivers standalone value. Phase 1 is immediately useful and validates the Meta API integration before building auto-publishing.

title: Google Drive over Dropbox for content intake
context: Original plan used Dropbox for raw content upload. Orien uses Google Drive.
decision: Use Google Drive API instead of Dropbox for the creative content pipeline
reasoning: Use what's already in the stack. No need to introduce a new tool.
